team.name: tech-leadership
team.header: Discussion and strategic planning for technological aspects of MediConn
team.purpose: To facilitate communication among the top tech executives of MediConn

team.name: product-development
team.header: Collaboration and discussion related to product development and improvements
team.purpose: To streamline communication and collaboration on product development

channel.name: capacity_planning
channel.header: Discussing capacity planning and scaling matters
channel.purpose: To coordinate and strategize on increasing concurrent user capacity

channel.name: competitor_analysis
channel.header: Discussing competitors' strategies, technologies, and capacities
channel.purpose: To understand and respond to competitive dynamics in the virtual care industry

post.message: Guys, we have a meeting with the design team tomorrow to discuss the UI/UX improvements for our healthcare app. Please prepare your ideas and suggestions.
We've seen a 20% increase in user traffic this month. Let's discuss how we can ensure our system's stability and performance during the peak hours.
Don't forget our meeting on maintenance and system upgrade schedules tomorrow. Let's ensure minimal downtime for our users.
I've completed the competitive analysis report for this quarter. Let's review it together next week and discuss our next steps.
I've received the updated hardware specs from our vendor. Let's discuss if we need to make any changes to our capacity planning.
Emily, let's also include user feedback in our competitor analysis. It will help in understanding the market better.
We're planning some network optimizations next week. I'll share the details soon so we can discuss the potential impact on our capacity.

As we've been analyzing our competitors' capacity, we've noticed that leading virtual-care platforms are supporting between 8,000-10,000 concurrent users. This is a significant gap compared to our peak capacity of 5,000 concurrent users in Q3 2025.

Thanks for sharing, Emily. This data highlights a scalability gap that we need to address. We must look into IT modernization efforts that can help us increase our capacity by at least 40% to match the projected patient-volume growth and stay aligned with national virtual care standards and shared digital health infrastructure by 2026.
